message:
perf: complete parking_lot migration and refine sync primitives

Finishes the std-to-parking_lot migration across all remaining modules,
eliminating lock-poison unwrap() overhead throughout the codebase. Also
includes targeted improvements to sync primitives that the migration
enabled.

parking_lot migration (bulk of changes):
- Remove .lock().unwrap() / .read().unwrap() / .write().unwrap() calls
  across gen_server, lab runtime, channels, combinators, distributed,
  net, transport, runtime scheduler/state/task_table, and all test/bench
  /example code
- parking_lot locks are non-poisoning, so the unwrap() calls were pure
  overhead that also obscured the actual lock operations in code review

Sync primitive improvements:
- rwlock: replace Arc<AtomicBool> waiter tracking with monotonic u64 IDs,
  simplify pop_writer_waiter/drain_reader_waiters to direct drain() calls
  now that cancelled waiters are handled via ID lookup instead of CAS on
  shared atomics
- cancel/symbol_cancel: switch children/listeners from Vec to SmallVec<2>
  to avoid heap allocation for the common 0-2 child/listener case; drain
  children under write lock with mem::take instead of cloning under read
- types/cancel: add CancelKind::as_str() for O(1) variant name lookup
- mutex: tighten notify wake path for parking_lot semantics
- deadline_monitor: simplify lock acquisition in tick/register paths
- blocking_pool/global_injector: remove redundant lock patterns
- arena: streamline slab allocation under parking_lot Mutex

Test updates:
- All 50+ test files updated for parking_lot API (no .unwrap() on locks)
- Benchmarks and examples updated consistently

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
